feedback.text: I have several points I would like bring up on this topic. Firstly it should be illegal to sell a game and not have the buyer own a copy of said game. Whether it be because the game runs on servers or because the buyer did not receive digital files of said game. Its preposterous that consumers are paying for a product they don't actually own after purchasing it. It should also be illegal to call it "Purchasing" when in reality the buyer is actually "Renting" the game for a timeframe. Its absurd that its legally okay for a company to sell me a product and then 7 years down the line tell me I can't access that product anymore, because they have shut down the servers, as if I didn't pay to own this product. Secondly it should not be legal for games that employ predatory gambling monetization to not be listed under gambling. Lootboxes etc are gambling and should be under the same laws as gambling. Anyone under 15 isn't allowed to play gambling games (slots etc.) so why should they be allowed to pay for a digital product that has a percentage on if the item received from it is a hit or a miss. Not to mention that digital product disappearing forever after that game is shutdown, therefore technically this should be called "donating" not a "purchase". There's a lot of conflicting verbage with lootboxes and "gacha" style games, that if made legally correct (ie. inline with other similar systems with similar verbage) would be blatantly gambling. Thirdly it is incredibly invasive and threatening to have to identify myself to prove I am of age online, when I'm using something that should not, in anyway, know any of this information about me. It also puts minors at risk of having their information leaked and sold, which in my mind is much more dangerous than adults going through the same. Its irresponsible and alarming that we are entering a world where its acceptable to force a minor to register their face, age and information online, for anything but government related matters. I understand wanting to protect minors from Adult Material, but this is not the answer to that. Thank you for hearing us out and I hope the European Union keeps being an inclusive, safe space where we can decide on matters as a society.
